cv_1771160797.74645.cv_content:
Adeola Azeez
azeez.risikat05@gmail.com | +447542891852 | Edinburgh, United Kingdom

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
I am an HR Generalist and Tech Recruiter with over six years of experience in talent acquisition, HR operations, and data-driven HR strategies. Passionate about leveraging HR analytics, automation, and digital transformation, I help organisations optimise recruitment processes, improve employee engagement, and drive business growth.

My expertise lies in:
 Talent Acquisition & Employer Branding – Designing data-backed sourcing strategies to attract top-tier talent.
 HR Analytics & Workforce Planning – Using HR metrics to inform hiring decisions and improve operational efficiencies.
 Employee Engagement & Culture Development – Enhancing workplace culture through strategic engagement initiatives.
 HR Digital Transformation – Implementing HRIS, ATS, and analytics tools to streamline HR processes.
Compliance & Policy Development – Ensuring organisations remain compliant while fostering an inclusive work environment.

Throughout my career, I have successfully reduced hiring time by 30%, developed HR analytics dashboards for real-time decision-making, and played a key role in implementing global HR policies. I thrive at the intersection of HR, data, and technology, constantly seeking innovative ways to transform HR operations.

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

HR Manager | Safeboda
2020-05-18 – 2022-12-30
1)Designed and implemented culture fit assessment tools for candidate evaluation, improving hiring quality and organisational alignment
2)Developed comprehensive succession planning framework to identify and address skill gaps across all organizational levels
3)Prepared detailed annual HR reports providing strategic insights on workforce trends, performance metrics, and organizational health
4)Created career progression grids aligned with unit-specific competency frameworks to track and guide employee development
5)Provided strategic HR advisory services to team leads, enhancing management effectiveness and decision-making
6)Led conflict resolution initiatives and managed grievance processes, maintaining positive employee relations
7)Coordinated performance management systems, driving continuous improvement in individual and team performance
8)Streamlined role definitions and responsibilities to facilitate vertical career movement within the organisation
9)Oversaw comprehensive employee performance management processes, ensuring consistent evaluation standards
• Launched a peer-to-peer learning programme to improve collaboration
• Introduced employee recognition programme such as employee of the month, birthday celebration etc  (20% improvement in employee experience)

Human Resources Officer | Reliance Infosystems
2018-02-05 – 2020-04-30
1) Collaborated on the development of comprehensive internal training materials for both new hires and existing staff, enhancing organizational learning capabilities
2) Successfully facilitated recruitment of two cohorts of Microsoft 4Afrika Interns, contributing to talent acquisition and corporate social responsibility initiatives
3) Designed and executed various employee engagement activities including team bonding sessions and sporting events, fostering workplace culture and team cohesion
4) Maintained accurate electronic records of salary advances, loans, and meal payment deductions, ensuring precise payroll computation and financial tracking
5) Implemented dual electronic and manual record-keeping systems for comprehensive employee data management, ensuring data integrity and accessibility
6)Created electronic interview scoresheets to standardize candidate evaluation processes and improve hiring consistency
• Digitally transformed HR process leading to the project launch of an Intranet 
• Introduced  digital annual Pulse check survey leading more efficient feedback collection  (with 60% increase in participation)

People Operations Manager | Bitpowr
 Collaborated with the information technology department to optimize data collection and analysis methodologies. 

 Developed recruitment strategies by providing comprehensive analyses of hiring trends.

Monitored and analysed salary structures, recommending adjustments to ensure market competitiveness.

Coordinated with external data providers to augment internal datasets and broaden analytical perspectives.

Coordinated with team leads for the development of Standard Operating Procedures to improve business and team efficiencies.

Led the development of Human Resources policies to elevate ways of working in the organisation.
• Increased headcount within 6  month resumption (50%)
• Improved employee statisfactions which led to 30% job satisfaction as reported by employees (30%)

SKILLS
People Management, Employee Relations, HR analytic, Performance Management, Employee Engagement & Culture Development, Compensation & Benefits, Strategic Development & Implementation, Microsoft PowerBI, Employment Law
cv_1771160797.74645.job_description:
Overview

InterSystems is seeking an experienced Human Resources Generalist with a strong focus on benefits and reward. The role will support HR Management in the continual development of company processes and procedures, benefit schemes as well as providing HR generalist support across the EMEA region. The ability to display a high level of discretion and confidentiality is essential at all times.

Responsibilities

Benefits and Reward Duties:

 Responsible for identifying, implementing, maintaining and communicating benefit schemes/services that are current, competitive and in compliance with local legislation across the EMEA region. 
Proactively manage benefit providers, brokers, benefit administration providers in the development and pricing analysis of benefit options and programmes, ensuring schemes/services meet the organisation and employees’ needs. 
Collect and analyse internal and external market data in order to prepare reports and support the continuous improvement of benefit and reward schemes. 
Continually review and assess in order to improve, streamline and enhance accuracy and process improvement. Support the delivery of core reward processes including annual salary review, job evaluation/job matching and benchmarking exercises. 
Prepare and review data as required. 
Ensure all benefit schemes/services are administered correctly and all required records are maintained. 
Produce other data analysis, reporting and presentations as required. 
Act as primary contact for all benefit queries in the region. 
Maintain strong relationships with local HR teams and Office Management to ensure the smooth running of the benefit schemes/services. 
Lead in-house benefit related projects and employee well-being initiatives and activities. 

Generalist Duties:

Prepare and administer all employee offer letters, employment contracts and contract changes. 
Manage and support the full HR cycle, including but not limited to on-boarding, off-boarding, absence management, probationary periods, employee queries, maternity, paternity and adoption requests. 
Implement all required administration, policies, procedures and communication for the employee lifecycle including accurate and timely maintenance of all HR systems and records. 
Administer and process candidate and employee DBS/background checks as required. 
Responsible for reporting worker location information to visa Sponsorship Management System. 
Prepare reports, including but not limited to, monthly headcount, absence reports and equity analysis. Work in collaboration with the HRIS Specialist to collate and manipulate data ready for presentation to senior management. 
Maintain up to date knowledge of employment law and compliance requirements. 
Maintain and update HR intranet site to ensure content is accurate and consistent. 
Work with the HR Business Partner and HR Management to support operational activity. 
Provide flexible coverage to HR colleagues during periods of absence. 
Undertake ad hoc requests and HR project work as required by the Director of Human Resources and Senior HR Manager. 

Qualifications

Essential

Minimum five years of experience within HR and currently your will be as a minimum a HR Generalist or more senior. 
Super-User / Extensive experience of Workday or other HCM system. 
Excellent communication skills complemented by high standards of work and presentation. 
Advanced Excel and strong MS Word and PowerPoint skills. 
Ability to build excellent working relationships with stakeholders to ensure they are proactively engaged with HR procedures and processes. 
Self-motivated, possess excellent planning skills and demonstrate high attention to detail, ensuring the Data Protection Act is adhered to at all times. 
Extensive experience of a fast-paced environment
Excellent employment legislation knowledge. 
To be able to work as part of a team and be results orientated. 

Education & Training

BS degree in a related field and/or a CIPD qualification would be an advantage but not essential

cv_1771528967.242081.job_description:
Role Responsibilities

Process, verify, and maintain personnel documentation including recruitment records, training files, grievances, performance evaluations, classifications, and leave records.
Record and validate employee data such as earnings, absences, production outputs, supervisory reports, and termination records.
Explain personnel policies, procedures, and benefits programs to employees and applicants while contributing knowledge to AI training datasets.
Assist in administering employee benefits and workers’ compensation programs while providing structured inputs for AI learning.
Respond to inquiries regarding eligibility, examinations, salaries, benefits, and HR policies with accurate documentation suitable for AI model training.
Collaborate with data annotation teams to structure HR scenarios and datasets for AI development.
Evaluate and refine AI-generated HR content to ensure compliance, accuracy, and alignment with HR best practices.


Requirements

Proven professional experience in human resources excluding payroll and timekeeping functions.
Strong understanding of employment law, HR policies, and employee benefits administration.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong attention to detail.
Demonstrated ability to process and organize complex HR and personnel information accurately.
Strong analytical skills with focus on data accuracy and integrity.
Comfortable using digital collaboration platforms and HR information systems in remote environments.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively across remote teams.
Experience contributing to AI or data annotation projects in HR is advantageous.
HR certifications such as SHRM or HRCI are preferred.
Experience in training delivery or change management initiatives is a plus.

cv_1769720610.348035.job_description:
About the Company

We are supporting a large, private equity–backed, multi‑site organisation operating at scale across multiple regions. The business is undergoing a period of strategic development and accelerated growth, with a strong emphasis on improving operational consistency, strengthening central functions and building robust frameworks that support long‑term success.


As a complex and fast‑moving organisation, it requires clear, structured and high‑quality communication to ensure alignment across numerous sites and stakeholders. The successful candidate will play an important role in shaping how the organisation communicates as it continues to grow and evolve.


The successful candidate will need to also have relevant experience in these areas.


Job Purpose

The People Advisor plays a key role within the People Services function, working closely with colleagues to deliver a high-quality, customer-focused service to managers and employees. The role provides expert advice and practical support across a range of people-related matters, ensuring consistency, compliance and professionalism in line with organisational policies and procedures.
This position is responsible for managing employee relations cases, supporting line managers through people processes, and responding to employee enquiries with sensitivity and accuracy.


Key Responsibilities

Provide end-to-end case management of employee relations matters
Monitor and manage short-term and long-term absence, including long-term sickness cases
Support the People Administration team by advising on escalated or complex issues
Implement and administer employee policies and procedures consistently
Contribute to the delivery of a high-performing operational People Service
Support the Senior People Manager with continuous improvement initiatives aligned to the People Strategy
Act as a trusted advisor to stakeholders across the organisation, delivering an excellent customer experience


Experience, Skills & Qualifications

Essential:

Experience working on complex ER matters at high volume
Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage high volumes of casework and meet deadlines
Proficient in Microsoft Word and Excel; numerate, accurate and efficient
Flexible, proactive and solution-focused approach
Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills
Ability to manage sensitive situations that may involve strong emotional responses
Knowledge of GDPR and confidentiality requirements
CIPD Certificate in Personnel Practice


Desirable:

Safeguarding experience

cv_1769720799.333419.cv_content:
Ajani Ajanlekoko
luffluv4real@gmail.com | +234809023456 | Edinburgh, United Kingdom

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
I am an HR Generalist and Tech Recruiter with over six years of experience in talent acquisition, HR operations, and data-driven HR strategies. Passionate about leveraging HR analytics, automation, and digital transformation, I help organisations optimise recruitment processes, improve employee engagement, and drive business growth.

My expertise lies in:
 Talent Acquisition & Employer Branding – Designing data-backed sourcing strategies to attract top-tier talent.
 HR Analytics & Workforce Planning – Using HR metrics to inform hiring decisions and improve operational efficiencies.
 Employee Engagement & Culture Development – Enhancing workplace culture through strategic engagement initiatives.
 HR Digital Transformation – Implementing HRIS, ATS, and analytics tools to streamline HR processes.
Compliance & Policy Development – Ensuring organisations remain compliant while fostering an inclusive work environment.

Throughout my career, I have successfully reduced hiring time by 30%, developed HR analytics dashboards for real-time decision-making, and played a key role in implementing global HR policies. I thrive at the intersection of HR, data, and technology, constantly seeking innovative ways to transform HR operations.

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

HR Operations Supervisor | Bitpowr Technologies
2022-01-03 – 2023-01-30
1) Collaborated with the information technology department to optimise data collection and analysis methodologies. 

2) Developed recruitment strategies by providing comprehensive analyses of hiring trends.

3)Monitored and analysed salary structures, recommending adjustments to ensure market competitiveness.

4)Coordinated with external data providers to augment internal datasets and broaden analytical perspectives.

5)Coordinated with team leads for the development of Standard Operating Procedures to improve business and team efficiencies.

6)Led the development of Human Resources policies to elevate ways of working in the organisation.

People Operations Supevisor | Safeboda
2020-03-02 – 2021-12-31
1)Designed and implemented culture fit assessment tools for candidate evaluation, improving hiring quality and organisational alignment
2)Developed comprehensive succession planning framework to identify and address skill gaps across all organizational levels
3)Prepared detailed annual HR reports providing strategic insights on workforce trends, performance metrics, and organizational health
4)Created career progression grids aligned with unit-specific competency frameworks to track and guide employee development
5)Provided strategic HR advisory services to team leads, enhancing management effectiveness and decision-making
6)Led conflict resolution initiatives and managed grievance processes, maintaining positive employee relations
7)Coordinated performance management systems, driving continuous improvement in individual and team performance
8)Streamlined role definitions and responsibilities to facilitate vertical career movement within the organisation
9)Oversaw comprehensive employee performance management processes, ensuring consistent evaluation standards

Junior HR Officer | Reliance Infosystems
2018-02-01 – 2020-02-28
1) Collaborated on the development of comprehensive internal training materials for both new hires and existing staff, enhancing organizational learning capabilities
2) Successfully facilitated recruitment of two cohorts of Microsoft 4Afrika Interns, contributing to talent acquisition and corporate social responsibility initiatives
3) Designed and executed various employee engagement activities including team bonding sessions and sporting events, fostering workplace culture and team cohesion
4) Maintained accurate electronic records of salary advances, loans, and meal payment deductions, ensuring precise payroll computation and financial tracking
5) Implemented dual electronic and manual record-keeping systems for comprehensive employee data management, ensuring data integrity and accessibility
6)Created electronic interview scoresheets to standardize candidate evaluation processes and improve hiring consistency

cv_1769720799.333419.job_description:
About the Company

We are supporting a large, private equity–backed, multi‑site organisation operating at scale across multiple regions. The business is undergoing a period of strategic development and accelerated growth, with a strong emphasis on improving operational consistency, strengthening central functions and building robust frameworks that support long‑term success.


As a complex and fast‑moving organisation, it requires clear, structured and high‑quality communication to ensure alignment across numerous sites and stakeholders. The successful candidate will play an important role in shaping how the organisation communicates as it continues to grow and evolve.


The successful candidate will need to also have relevant experience in these areas.


Job Purpose

The People Advisor plays a key role within the People Services function, working closely with colleagues to deliver a high-quality, customer-focused service to managers and employees. The role provides expert advice and practical support across a range of people-related matters, ensuring consistency, compliance and professionalism in line with organisational policies and procedures.
This position is responsible for managing employee relations cases, supporting line managers through people processes, and responding to employee enquiries with sensitivity and accuracy.


Key Responsibilities

Provide end-to-end case management of employee relations matters
Monitor and manage short-term and long-term absence, including long-term sickness cases
Support the People Administration team by advising on escalated or complex issues
Implement and administer employee policies and procedures consistently
Contribute to the delivery of a high-performing operational People Service
Support the Senior People Manager with continuous improvement initiatives aligned to the People Strategy
Act as a trusted advisor to stakeholders across the organisation, delivering an excellent customer experience


Experience, Skills & Qualifications

Essential:

Experience working on complex ER matters at high volume
Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage high volumes of casework and meet deadlines
Proficient in Microsoft Word and Excel; numerate, accurate and efficient
Flexible, proactive and solution-focused approach
Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills
Ability to manage sensitive situations that may involve strong emotional responses
Knowledge of GDPR and confidentiality requirements
CIPD Certificate in Personnel Practice


Desirable:

Safeguarding experience
